返回本批次

打破控制台：视频游戏安全简史

Breaking the console: a brief history of video game security

sergioprado.blog 作者 sprado 分数 89 评论 26 2026/04/07 17:51
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 随着游戏机发展成为具有多层保护的成熟计算平台，视频游戏安全性一直是一个不断变化的目标——但对于每一个发明的锁，总是有人决心撬开它。作为一名视频游戏迷，我从未过多考虑过这些问题，直到我成为一名工程师并开始深入研究安全性。游戏机的核心是嵌入式系统，其安全性的建立、破坏和重建的历史充满了教训，这些教训远远超出了我们的客厅。在本文中，我们将回顾视频游戏机安全的历史，从几乎没有任何保护的早期，到几十年来日益复杂的防御，再到使用许多与安全敏感的嵌入式设备中相同的技术的现代系统，同时展示尽管如此，研究人员和爱好者如何不断寻找方法。我将主要关注家用游戏机而不是手持系统，其中任天堂 Switch 是一个例外，因为它位于这两个类别的交叉点。为了使本文保持合理的长度，我不会尝试涵盖每个已发布的漏洞或利用。相反，我将重点关注一些最具代表性和流行的例子。出于同样的原因，我不会详细介绍每次攻击的完整技术细节，但我会尽可能为想要深入挖掘的读者提供参考和链接。我的目标是展示视频游戏行业的安全性如何演变，以及我们可以从这种演变中学到什么。最后，无论我们是设计视频游戏机、医疗设备还是工业系统，威胁模型可能有所不同，但许多潜在的安全挑战非常相似。那么让我们深入了解一下吧！狂野的西部：门上没有锁的时候 最早的家用游戏机，如 Atari 2600 (1977)，基本上没有安全保障。硬件没有机制来验证盒带是否包含合法软件。任何连接到正确连接器的 ROM 芯片都可以运行。唯一真正的障碍是物理和经济：您需要硬件来制造墨盒。
  2. 来源：维基百科 Atari 2600 时代是游戏界的狂野西部。没有代码签名，没有加密验证，也没有有意的区域锁定方案——尽管地区 NTSC/PAL/SECAM 差异仍然影响兼容性。像动视这样的第三方发行商是由雅达利工程师创立的，他们离开去制作自己的游戏，因为技术上没有什么可以阻止他们这样做。雅达利唯一的选择是采取法律行动，而不是技术控制。随着 1985 年任天堂娱乐系统 (NES) 的出现，这种情况发生了变化，它首次认真尝试了硬件强制软件控制。这是如何运作的，持续了多长时间？硬件锁定芯片和 10NES 1983 年北美视频游戏崩盘给任天堂带来了伤痕累累，部分原因是市场上充斥着劣质游戏，任天堂在 NES 上配备了一个名为 10NES（后来称为 CIC，或检查集成电路）的专用安全 IC，该 IC 存在于游戏机和每个授权卡带中。
  3. 资料来源：维基百科 该芯片是一个质询-响应身份验证系统：控制台内的 10NES 芯片将与每个许可卡带内匹配的 10NES 芯片进行通信。如果身份验证握手失败，控制台将不断重置（每秒一次），从而产生臭名昭著的闪烁屏幕。
  4. 来源：Fabien Sanglards 网站 这两款芯片都是相同的 4 位微控制器 (Sharp SM590)，运行相同的固件，但以不同的角色运行，通过上面原理图中的 CONF 引脚进行配置。控制台芯片作为主芯片（CONF=1），盒芯片作为从芯片（CONF=0）。加电后，两个芯片通过共享时钟和数据线进行同步，并开始交换由内部移位寄存器生成的伪随机位序列。控制台芯片驱动时钟并不断将墨盒芯片的输出与其自身的预期序列进行比较。只要两个芯片保持同步（这意味着卡带包含运行相同程序的正品 10NES 芯片），身份验证就会成功并且控制台会正常启动。如果序列在任何点出现分歧，控制台芯片就会将重置线拉低，从而导致重置（并闪烁屏幕）。至关重要的是，不涉及密钥——两种芯片都运行完全相同的算法，而安全性完全依赖于该算法是未知的且难以从硅中提取。这是最纯粹形式的通过默默无闻实现的安全！这也是基于硬件的软件身份验证在消费设备中的首次广泛使用。它有效地阻止了未经许可的卡带——至少在一段时间内是这样，因为 10NES 的逆向工程相对较快…… Atari 的游戏发行子公司 Tengen 以对芯片进行逆向工程并制造了自己的克隆（Rabbit）来生产未经许可的 NES 卡带而闻名。是的，你没有看错，雅达利入侵了任天堂！这导致了具有里程碑意义的雅达利游戏公司诉美国任天堂案（1992 年）。与此同时，自制软件社区发现，通过切断控制台内芯片上的特定引脚来物理禁用 CIC 芯片，可以防止其将重置线拉低，从而允许控制台启动，无论插入什么卡带。一些未经许可的制造商走得更远，在 CIC 拉动重置线之前，使用电压尖峰关闭身份验证过程中的 CIC——这是故障注入攻击的早期、粗略的例子，同一类硬件技术后来被用来击败 Xbox 360 的加密启动链。任天堂在三代基于卡带的游戏机（NES、Super NES 和 Nintendo 64）中采用了 CIC 方法，每次迭代都会改进芯片，使其更难克隆。但根本的弱点永远不会改变，因为身份验证纯粹基于兼容芯片的物理存在，没有对实际游戏代码的加密验证。一旦你理解了协议，你就可以克隆或绕过芯片。直到 GameCube (2001) 放弃了专有迷你 DVD 的卡带后，任天堂才最终放弃了 CIC 模式。这个时代建立了一种会重复数十年的模式：基于默默无闻或硬件可用性的安全措施，然后是逆向工程，然后是旁路。向光学媒体的转变如何改变了游戏规则？光盘和模组芯片的诞生 PlayStation (1994) 从盒式磁带转向 CD-ROM，索尼知道，日益便宜的 CD-R 驱动器使盗版成为严重威胁。因此，它创建了一个以两个相互关联的想法为中心的复制保护方案：驱动器固件寻找在光盘内部附近的非标准摆动区域中编码的特殊区域特定 SCEx 身份验证信号，并且光盘的区域/许可标识符必须与控制台的市场相匹配。从本质上讲，整个保护依赖于普通消费者 CD 写入器无法复制的自定义媒体格式。这引入了新的攻击面，并帮助普及了一种称为 modchip 的新型硬件修改。典型的 PlayStation 模组芯片是焊接在主板上的小型微控制器，它会欺骗 SCEx 身份验证序列，从而允许驱动器接受复制的或区域外的光盘。
  5. 资料来源：Kevin Chung 的博客 在游戏机推出后的一年内，出现了数十种模组芯片设计，其中许多只需要将几根电线焊接到主板上的特定焊盘上即可。除了 modchips 之外，PlayStation 还容易受到臭名昭著的交换技巧的影响：使用合法光盘启动，保持盖子传感器打开，并在初始身份验证检查后交换刻录的副本。更广泛的教训是，PlayStation 的主要保护位于光盘验证层，没有对加载到内存中的代码进行加密验证。 CPU 将执行光盘中包含的任何内容。绕过磁盘检查足以运行任何东西。 PlayStation 2 (2000) 通过更强大的驱动器身份验证方案稍微提高了标准，但最终仍然可以通过 modchips 和交换技巧来击败。这种仅采用光盘的安全方法并不是索尼独有的：Sega Saturn 依赖于类似的基于光盘的身份验证，甚至任天堂 GameCube 也使用专有的 miniDVD 格式作为其主要屏障。这些控制台都没有尝试以加密方式验证它们执行的代码——保护在光驱处停止。通过光盘检查后，CPU 不再提出任何问题。 Sega Dreamcast 值得单独提及，因为它的保护不仅仅依赖于简单的光盘验证检查。它结合了专有的 GD-ROM 格式、对 MIL-CD 格式的支持以及可执行的加扰方案。实际上，当爱好者学会滥用 MIL-CD 启动路径并重现加扰逻辑，允许软件从普通 CD-R 启动时，控制台就会受到损害。最终，保护媒体永远是不够的。下一步是保护代码本身。加密代码签名进入游戏机最初的 Xbox (2001) 是在熟悉的 PC 硬件（Pentium III 衍生品、Nvidia GPU、标准硬盘驱动器）上构建的，运行源自 Windows 2000 的精简操作系统。它是最早实现植根于 MCPX 内隐藏启动块的加密信任链的主要家用游戏机之一。该引导块解密并验证了外部引导加载程序，而外部引导加载程序又解密并验证了内核。然后内核对其加载的软件强制进行签名检查。 Andrew “bunnie” Huang 是最早对 Xbox 安全架构进行逆向工程的研究人员之一，他在 2002 年的麻省理工学院备忘录中记录了他的工作，后来又在 Hacking the Xbox：逆向工程简介中记录了他的工作 - 是的，它是免费下载的，所以去获取一份吧！ 😉 他展示了 Xbox 的隐藏启动 ROM 可以通过点击 HyperTransport 总线来恢复，因为启动代码以明确的方式遍历该总线。这样做不需要打开芯片，但确实需要定制基于 FPGA 的总线嗅探硬件和对主板的物理访问。

Original Summary

  1. Video game security has always been a moving target, as consoles evolved to full-blown computing platforms locked down with layers of protection — but for every lock ever invented, there has always been someone determined to pick it. Being a video game fan myself, I never gave much thought to any of this until I became an engineer and started diving into security. Consoles are embedded systems at their core, and the history of how their security was built, broken, and rebuilt is full of lessons that apply well beyond our living rooms. In this article, we will walk through the history of video game console security, from the early days when there was virtually no protection at all, through decades of increasingly sophisticated defenses, and up to modern systems that use many of the same techniques found in security-sensitive embedded devices — while showing how, despite all of that, researchers and enthusiasts kept finding ways in. I will focus primarily on home consoles rather than handheld systems, with the Nintendo Switch included as an exception because it sits at the intersection of both categories. To keep this article to a reasonable length, I will not attempt to cover every published vulnerability or exploit. Instead, I will focus on some of the most representative and popular examples. For the same reason, I will not go into full technical detail on every attack, but I will provide references and links where possible for readers who want to dig deeper. My goal is to show how security evolved across the video game industry and what we can learn from that evolution. In the end, whether we are designing a video game console, a medical device, or an industrial system, the threat model may differ, but many of the underlying security challenges are remarkably similar. So let’s dive in! The wild west: when there was no lock on the door The earliest home consoles, like the Atari 2600 (1977), had essentially no security. The hardware had no mechanism to verify whether a cartridge contained legitimate software. Any ROM chip wired to the right connector would run. The only real barrier was physical and economic: you needed the hardware to manufacture a cartridge.
  2. Source: Wikipedia The Atari 2600 era was the wild west of gaming. There was no code signing, no cryptographic verification, and no intentional region-locking scheme — though regional NTSC/PAL/SECAM differences still affected compatibility. Third-party publishers like Activision were famously founded by Atari engineers who left to make their own games, because nothing technically stopped them from doing so. Atari’s only option was legal action, not technical controls. This changed with the arrival of the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES) in 1985, which introduced the first serious attempt at hardware-enforced software control. How did that work, and how long did it last? Hardware lockout chips and the 10NES Scarred by the 1983 North American video game crash, partly caused by a market flooded with poor-quality titles, Nintendo shipped the NES with a dedicated security IC called the 10NES (later known as the CIC, or Checking Integrated Circuit), present in both the console and every licensed cartridge.
  3. Source: Wikipedia The chip was a challenge-response authentication system: a 10NES chip inside the console would communicate with a matching 10NES chip inside every licensed cartridge. If the authentication handshake failed, the console would continuously reset (once per second), producing the infamous blinking screen.
  4. Source: Fabien Sanglards’s website Both chips were identical 4-bit microcontrollers (Sharp SM590) running the same firmware, but operating in different roles, configured via the CONF pin in the schematics above. The console chip acted as the master (CONF=1), the cartridge chip as the slave (CONF=0). On power-on, the two chips synchronized over a shared clock and data line and began exchanging a pseudo-random bit sequence generated by an internal shift register. The console chip drove the clock and continuously compared the cartridge chip’s output against its own expected sequence. As long as both chips stayed in sync - meaning the cartridge contained a genuine 10NES chip running the same program - authentication succeeded and the console booted normally. If the sequences diverged at any point, the console chip pulled the reset line low, causing the reset (and blinking screen). Crucially, there was no secret key involved — both chips ran the exact same algorithm, and the security relied entirely on that algorithm being unknown and difficult to extract from silicon. This is security through obscurity in its purest form! It was also the first widespread use of hardware-based software authentication in a consumer device. And it effectively locked out unlicensed cartridges - at least for a while, because the 10NES was reverse-engineered relatively quickly… Tengen, Atari’s game publishing subsidiary, famously reverse-engineered the chip and manufactured their own clone (Rabbit) to produce unlicensed NES cartridges. Yes, you read that right, Atari hacked Nintendo! This led to the landmark lawsuit Atari Games Corp. v. Nintendo of America (1992). The homebrew community, meanwhile, discovered that physically disabling the CIC chip by cutting specific pins on the chip inside the console would prevent it from pulling the reset line low, allowing the console to boot regardless of what cartridge was inserted. Some unlicensed manufacturers went further, using a voltage spike to shut down the CIC mid-authentication before it could pull the reset line — an early, crude example of a fault injection attack, the same class of hardware technique that would later be used to defeat the Xbox 360’s cryptographic boot chain. Nintendo carried the CIC approach across three generations of cartridge-based consoles — the NES, the Super NES, and the Nintendo 64 — refining the chip with each iteration to make it harder to clone. But the fundamental weakness never change, as the authentication was purely based on the physical presence of a compatible chip, with no cryptographic verification of the actual game code. Once you understood the protocol, you could clone or bypass the chip. It was only with the GameCube (2001), which abandoned cartridges for proprietary miniDVDs, that Nintendo finally left the CIC model behind. This era established a pattern that would repeat itself for decades: a security measure based on obscurity or hardware availability, followed by reverse engineering, followed by a bypass. How did the transition to optical media change the game? Optical discs and the birth of the modchip The PlayStation (1994) moved from cartridges to CD-ROMs, and Sony knew that increasingly affordable CD-R drives made piracy a serious threat. So it created a copy-protection scheme centered on two linked ideas: the drive firmware looked for a special region-specific SCEx authentication signal encoded in a non-standard wobble area near the inner part of the disc and the disc’s region/licensing identifiers had to match the console’s market. In essence, the whole protection relied on a custom media format that ordinary consumer CD writers could not reproduce. This introduced a new attack surface and helped popularize a new class of hardware modifications known as modchips. A typical PlayStation modchip was a small microcontroller soldered onto the motherboard that spoofed the SCEx authentication sequence, allowing the drive to accept copied or out-of-region discs.
  5. Source: Kevin Chung’s blog Dozens of modchip designs appeared within a year of the console’s launch, many requiring nothing more than soldering a few wires to specific pads on the board. Beyond modchips, the PlayStation was also vulnerable to the infamous swap trick: boot with a legitimate disc, hold the lid sensor open, and swap in a burned copy after the initial authentication check. The broader lesson is that the PlayStation’s main protection lived at the disc-authentication layer, with no cryptographic verification of the code being loaded into memory. The CPU would execute whatever the disc contained. Bypassing the disc check was sufficient to run anything. The PlayStation 2 (2000) raised the bar slightly with a more robust drive authentication scheme, but it was ultimately still defeatable with modchips and the swap trick. This disc-only approach to security was not unique to Sony: the Sega Saturn relied on similar disc-based authentication, and even the Nintendo GameCube used a proprietary miniDVD format as its primary barrier. None of these consoles attempted to cryptographically verify the code they executed — protection stopped at the optical drive. Get past the disc check, and the CPU asked no further questions. The Sega Dreamcast deserves a separate mention, as its protection did not rely solely on a simple disc-authentication check. It combined the proprietary GD-ROM format, support for the MIL-CD format, and an executable scrambling scheme. In practice, the console was compromised when enthusiasts learned to abuse the MIL-CD boot path and reproduce the scrambling logic, allowing software to boot from ordinary CD-Rs. In the end, protecting the media was never going to be enough. The next step was to protect the code itself. Cryptographic code signing enters the console The original Xbox (2001) was built on familiar PC hardware (Pentium III derivative, Nvidia GPU, standard hard drive), running a stripped-down OS derived from Windows 2000. It was one of the earliest major home consoles to implement a cryptographic chain of trust rooted in a hidden boot block inside the MCPX. That boot block decrypted and verified an external bootloader, which in turn decrypted and verified the kernel. The kernel then enforced signature checks on the software it loaded. Andrew “bunnie” Huang was one of the earliest and most influential researchers to reverse-engineer the Xbox security architecture, documenting his work in a 2002 MIT memo and later in Hacking the Xbox: An Introduction to Reverse Engineering — and yes, it is free to download, so go grab a copy! 😉 He showed that the Xbox’s hidden boot ROM could be recovered by tapping the HyperTransport bus, because the boot code traversed that bus in the clear. Doing so did not require decapping the chip, but it did require custom FPGA-based bus-sniffing hardware and physical access to the motherboard.

评论（默认中文）

wise0wl 2026/04/07 22:15
所以，我不知道这是人工智能生成的还是作者实际上不知道，但雅达利盒式磁带和软盘通常都有复制保护。 我叔叔当时活跃在现场，作为一名电气工程师想出了一个解决方案。 当我在 90 年代继承他的 Atari 800 时，软盘驱动器上有一个物理按钮，可以在写入时将坏扇区强制写入磁盘。 他有关于每场比赛这些坏扇区出现时间的笔记本。

所以，是的。 这篇关于“狂野西部”雅达利时代的“文章”几乎从一开始就是不正确的。

So, I don't know if this is AI generated or whether the author is actually unaware, but Atari cartridges and floppies commonly had copy protections. My uncle was active in the scene at the time, and as an electrical engineer came up with a solution. When I inherited his Atari 800 in the 90s there was a physical button wired into the floppy drive which would force a bad sector onto the disk as it was being written. He had notebooks about the timing for these bad sectors per game.

So, yeah. The "article" is incorrect from nearly the get-go about the "wild west" Atari age.

在 HN 中查看
sprado 2026/04/07 22:39
感谢您的评论。

不，它不是人工智能生成的。这是基于我的研究。

我认为雅达利家用游戏机和雅达利家用电脑之间存在混淆。

在该部分中，我谈论的是早期的游戏机平台，例如 Atari 2600，其中卡带接口本身没有锁定/身份验证机制，与任天堂后来在 10NES 上所做的类似。这就是为什么第三方卡带可能存在，而雅达利的主要反应是合法的而不是技术上的。

您对 Atari 800 的描述是真实的，但它属于不同的环境：Atari 8 位计算机系列，尤其是软盘软件，其中故意坏扇区和基于时间的检查等复制保护技巧确实很常见。

因此，我同意雅达利计算机软件经常使用复制保护，但这与我关于早期游戏机时代的观点并不矛盾。

Thanks for the comment.

No, it is not AI generated. It was based on my research.

I think there is a mix-up here between Atari home consoles and Atari home computers.

In that section I was talking about early console platforms such as the Atari 2600, where the cartridge interface itself had no lockout/authentication mechanism comparable to what Nintendo later did with the 10NES. That is why third-party cartridges could exist and Atari’s main response was legal rather than technical.

What you describe for the Atari 800 is real, but it belongs to a different context: the Atari 8-bit computer line, especially floppy-disk software, where copy-protection tricks such as intentional bad sectors and timing-based checks were indeed common.

So I agree that Atari computer software often used copy protection, but that does not contradict the point I was making about the early console era.

在 HN 中查看
landr0id 2026/04/08 08:37
您好，关于“对于现代 Xbox 平台，2024 年公开工作暴露了 Xbox One 和 Xbox Series 上的 SystemOS 内核利用”的快速说明

我是前 Xbox 黑客，然后是前 Microsoft 员工，（很久之后）离开 Microsoft，帮助解决了附带损害后利用有效负载。

Xbox One 安全性的设计早于我，但微软一直都知道 SystemOS 将是一个薄弱环节，几乎肯定会受到损害，并将大部分可以受到轻微攻击的攻击面推到那里。系统shell、第3方应用程序、指南等都在SystemOS中运行。

他们关注的关键问题是：

1、极强的纵深防御

2. 使全部或部分开发变得不经济

第 3 方应用程序和 Web 浏览器被视为明显不受信任且需要 JIT，因为它们主要基于 .NET 或 JS VM。但实际上，该虚拟机应该没有什么有趣的地方：它的妥协不应导致盗版/作弊，并且理想情况下不应泄露游戏明文。

但其他一些人发现，由于某种原因，明文实际上对 SystemOS 是可见的，但并没有在控制台上启用盗版。不过，您可以使用 XWine1 在 PC 上运行这些游戏： https://github.com/xwine1

从技术上讲，除了动机之外，没有理由认为附带损害不会在 Xbox One 生命周期的早期发生。即使如此，您也可能会经历一些 Hyper-V Nday 并危及 HostOS。

多年来还出现了其他“漏洞”：有些人设法通过云连接存储和其他恶作剧、系统 shell 中的 XSS（其中一些应用程序是 JS）等来篡改游戏保存，但其中大多数都是相对良性且易于修补的。还有一小群人与科拉有着类似但能力较弱的人。

Collat​​ 允许泄露明文。

幸福打破了一切:)

Hi, quick note on "For modern Xbox platforms, public 2024 work exposed SystemOS kernel exploitation on both Xbox One and Xbox Series"

I'm a former Xbox hacker, then former Microsoft employee, and (long after) leaving Microsoft helped with the Collateral Damage post-exploitation payload.

The design of the Xbox One security predates me, but Microsoft has always known that SystemOS would be a weak link that would almost guaranteed to be compromised and shoved most of their attack surface that can be trivially attacked in there. The system shell, 3rd-party apps, guide, etc. all run in SystemOS.

The key things they focused on though were:

1. Extremely strong defense-in-depth

2. Making full or partial exploitation not economical

3rd party apps and the web browser were seen as being obviously untrusted _and_ needed JIT because they'd mostly be based on .NET or the JS VM. But practically speaking there should be nothing interesting in that VM: its compromise shouldn't enable piracy/cheating and ideally shouldn't leak game plaintext.

What some others found though was that for some reason plaintext was actually visible to SystemOS, but didn't enable piracy on console. You can take those games though and run them on PC using XWine1: https://github.com/xwine1

Technically speaking there's no reason why Collateral Damage couldn't have happened waayyyyy earlier in the Xbox One's lifecycle except for motivation. Even still you could probably take some Hyper-V N-day and compromise HostOS through.

Over there years there have been other "exploits" too: some folks have managed to tamper with gamesaves via cloud connected storage and other shenanigans, XSS in the system shell (some of these apps are JS), etc., but most of this was relatively benign and easily patchable. And there has been a very, very small group of people with similar but less capable exploits to Collat.

Collat allowed compromise of plaintext.

Bliss breaks everything :)

在 HN 中查看
Pannoniae 2026/04/08 00:02
还有另一个不正确的事实：“最初的 Xbox (2001) 是基于熟悉的 PC 硬件构建的（Pentium III 衍生产品、Intel GPU、标准硬盘）”

（这是一个 NV GPU）

Also another incorrect factoid: "The original Xbox (2001) was built on familiar PC hardware (Pentium III derivative, Intel GPU, standard hard drive)"

(it was an NV GPU)

在 HN 中查看
sprado 2026/04/08 02:05
很好的收获。事实上，GPU 是 Nvidia 的 NV2A，而不是英特尔 GPU。我会在文章中纠正这一点。感谢您指出。
Good catch. Indeed the GPU was Nvidia’s NV2A, not an Intel GPU. I will correct that in the article. Thanks for pointing it out.
在 HN 中查看
gjsman-1000 2026/04/07 23:12
我觉得有趣的是，早在 1985 年，在 Atari 与 NES 的对比中，我们就有证据表明消费者更喜欢围墙花园。围墙花园从一个完全死寂的市场中爆炸出来，而已经存在的开放系统则自杀了。苹果公司继续在这个现实上大赚一笔，微软发明了一个伪围墙花园，这已经成为技术死胡同，而自由和开源软件社区仍然否认事情不应该是这样的，而不是接受现实并通过强制规则发明自己策划的体验。
I find it interesting that all the way back in 1985, in Atari vs NES, we had proof that consumers preferred walled gardens. The walled garden exploded from a completely dead market, while the already-existing open system killed itself. Apple proceeded to make a killing of their own on this reality, Microsoft invented a pseudo-walled garden that has become a technical dead end, while FOSS communities are still in denial about how things shouldn't be that way rather than accepting reality and inventing their own curated experience with enforced rules.
在 HN 中查看
garciansmith 2026/04/08 00:24
我不同意，这不是关于消费者，而是关于其他企业。任天堂在美国采取的围墙花园方法需要说服零售商再次在商店货架上库存视频游戏。当然，Famicom 并没有采用同样的方法，尽管任天堂讨厌第三方可以轻松制造 Famicom 手推车，但该系统的开放性肯定不会损害它在日本的表现。
I disagree, it wasn't about consumers, but rather other businesses. The walled garden approach Nintendo took in America was needed to convince retailers to stock video games on store shelves again. And of course the Famicom didn't have that same approach, and while Nintendo hated the fact third parties could easily make Famicom carts, the open nature of the system certainly didn't hurt it in Japan.
在 HN 中查看
RajT88 2026/04/07 23:20
> 微软发明伪围墙花园，已成技术死胡同

如果您指的是 Windows，那么这根本就不是很严格的。 你几乎不需要一个 电脑 编写和发布 Windows 应用程序，更不用说赚钱了。

也许是办公室？ 或者各种其他产品。

> Microsoft invented a pseudo-walled garden that has become a technical dead end

If you're referring to Windows, this is not very walled at all. You barely need a computer to write and release windows apps, let alone money.

Office, perhaps? Or a variety of other products.

在 HN 中查看
gjsman-1000 2026/04/07 23:23
Windows 是一个面向开发人员的开放平台……如果您忽略所有安全检查和 Windows Defender，那么这个停滞的平台在本机工具方面全面落后于其他平台约 20 年（例如，我应该使用哪个 UI 框架，它好吗？）。

然而，Windows 上还固定有很多很多围墙花园的东西。未经 Microsoft 批准，您不得分发自己的驱动程序。您没有在 Wine 上运行 Microsoft Office。未经 Microsoft 许可，您无法连接到 Active Directory。您没有制定适用于 Linux 的 MDM 组策略。您根本就不是在制造 Windows 设备，除非它们满足 Microsoft 的系统要求和要求（例如键盘上的 Windows 图标）。您的 BIOS 必须遵循有关激活​​密钥融合位置的严格规则。 ETC。

在这方面，Windows 只能从 最终用户 看法。其他方面都是封闭的，封闭的 紧紧。

Windows is an open platform for developers... if you ignore all of the security checks and Windows Defender and the stagnant platform which is about 2 decades behind everyone else, across the board, in terms of native tooling (e.g. which UI framework should I use and is it good?).

However, Windows also has many, many, walled garden things bolted onto it. You aren't distributing your own drivers without Microsoft's approval. You aren't running Microsoft Office on Wine. You aren't connecting to Active Directory without Microsoft's blessing. You aren't making group policies that work on Linux for MDM. You aren't manufacturing Windows devices, at all, unless they meet Microsoft's system requirements and mandates (e.g. a Windows icon on the keyboard). Your BIOS must follow strict rules about where the activation key is fused. Etc.

In that respect, Windows is only open from an end user perspective. In all other respects, it is closed, and it is closed tightly.

在 HN 中查看
RajT88 2026/04/08 00:31
> 未经 Microsoft 批准，您不得分发自己的驱动程序。

仅内核驱动程序。

> 未经 Microsoft 许可，您无法连接到 Active Directory。

我想你说的是 EntraID。 这确实是事实。 您只需启动 Windows Server 并创建域控制器即可，没问题。 不过，您不需要 Microsoft 来提供域服务 - 您可以使用其他域控制器类型。 （你不会得到 GPO 和其他东西 - 这不是一个“围墙花园”的东西，这是其他系统没有的功能集）

> 在这方面，Windows 仅从最终用户的角度开放。其他方面都是封闭的，而且封闭得很严实。

并不像你想象的那么紧。 无论如何，我特别指的是构建 Windows 应用程序 - 你并不反对这一点。 您绝对可以下载各种免费工具，构建应用程序，将其打包为 .zip 或 .msi 并从各个地方分发它。 Windows 应用程序商店是一个有围墙的花园，但您不必使用它。

> You aren't distributing your own drivers without Microsoft's approval.

Only kernel drivers.

> You aren't connecting to Active Directory without Microsoft's blessing.

I think you're talking about EntraID. That is true enough. You can just spin up Windows Server and create a domain controller, no problem. You don't need Microsoft for domain services, though - you can use other domain controller types. (You don't get GPO and other things - that's not a 'walled garden' thing, that's a feature set which other systems don't have)

> In that respect, Windows is only open from an end user perspective. In all other respects, it is closed, and it is closed tightly.

Not so tight as you seem to think. And anyways, I was specifically referring to building windows apps - which you did not disagree with. You absolutely can pull down various free tools, build an app, package it up as a .zip or .msi and distribute it from a variety of places. The Windows app store is a walled garden, but you don't have to use it.

在 HN 中查看
mywittyname 2026/04/08 00:33
我认为要点是消费者希望相信他们购买的产品与广告中的一样。
I think the takeaway is consumers want to trust the products they are buying are as advertised.
在 HN 中查看
gosub100 2026/04/07 23:41
我认为消费者选择了质量和便利。碰巧，围墙花园是实现这一目标的最简单方法。电子产品，尤其是计算机，当时非常昂贵。我不能责怪人们购买了一台能用的游戏机。直到 90 年代末，兼容性仍然是一个问题，因为很多人不知道计算机是如何工作的。
I think consumers chose quality and convenience. It just so happens that the walled garden is the easiest way to accomplish this. Electronics, especially computers, were extremely expensive back then. I can't blame people for buying a console that just works. Compatibility was an issue well into the late 90s because so many people didn't know how computers worked.
在 HN 中查看
sprado 2026/04/07 17:51
一段时间没有在博客上发帖后，我决定重新开始——休息后我的第一篇文章是关于视频游戏安全的历史！一路上也有一些精彩的故事，比如雅达利对任天堂的锁定系统进行逆向工程，或者简单地改变林克马的名字如何成为任天堂 Wii 上的攻击媒介。我在研究和撰写这篇文章时获得了很多乐趣，并且在这个过程中学到了很多东西。我希望你也喜欢它！
After some time without posting on my blog, I decided to get back to it — and my first post after the break is about the history of video game security! There are also some great stories along the way, like Atari reverse-engineering Nintendo’s lockout system, or how simply changing the name of Link's horse became an attack vector on the Nintendo Wii. I had a lot of fun researching and writing this article, and I learned a lot in the process. I hope you enjoy it too!
在 HN 中查看
djmips 2026/04/08 00:11
谢谢我确实喜欢它！有一件事是，我觉得 PS1 摆动光盘保护虽然通过交换和 mod 芯片绕过，但除了索尼以外的其他人从未可能（除了一个历史例子：Datal）在控制台上制作可引导的光盘。我觉得大多数 PS1 都没有经过改装，所以这是一个做得很好的锁定。但我知道这不是你的主要观点 - 只是它是一种令人惊讶的有效复制保护。
Thanks I did enjoy it! One thing is, I feel like the PS1 wobble disc protection, although bypassed with swaps and mod chips, it has never been possible (besides one historic example: Datel) for someone other than Sony to make a disc bootable on the console. I feel like most PS1's weren't modded so it was quite a well done lockout. But I understand that wasn't your main point - just that it was a surprisingly effective copy protection.
在 HN 中查看
favorited 2026/04/08 02:37
作为消费者，10NES 设计的最佳部分是它是故障安全的，而不是故障安全的。控制台默认启动。因此，如果您的 NES 开始出现臭名昭著的启动重置闪烁灯，您所要做的就是拧下 NES 外壳并将电源引脚夹在 10NES 芯片上。这些都是巨大的别针，不需要任何微妙之处。你可以用指甲刀来做。
The best part of the 10NES design, as a consumer, was that it was fail-safe, rather than fail-secure. The console defaulted to booting. So, if your NES started having the infamous boot-reset flashing light, all you had to do was unscrew the NES enclosure and clip the power pin on the 10NES chip. And these were huge pins, it didn't require any subtlety. You could do it with a nail clipper.
在 HN 中查看
bob1029 2026/04/07 22:25
现代控制台在使用 PKI 和证书的方式方面已接近完美。即使您克隆了有效控制台的加密身份，供应商也可以快速检测到这种不可能的访问场景。
The modern consoles are pretty close to perfect with how they use PKI and certificates. Even if you clone the cryptographic identity of a valid console, the vendor can quickly detect this impossible access scenario.
在 HN 中查看
sprado 2026/04/07 22:48
正确的。如今，身份管理非常重要，大多数与安全相关的认证和法规都需要它。
Correct. And identity management is so important nowadays that most security-related certifications and regulations require it.
在 HN 中查看
riverforest 2026/04/07 20:36
游戏机制造商和黑客之间的猫捉老鼠是科技领域最诚实的故事之一。双方都在不断让对方变得更好。
The cat and mouse between console makers and hackers is one of the more honest stories in tech. Both sides kept making each other better.
在 HN 中查看
sprado 2026/04/07 21:16
真的！
True!
在 HN 中查看
cheeseomlit 2026/04/07 22:20
我记得那天我哥哥得到了一块 PS1 modchip，但它不需要任何焊接 - 你只需将它插入控制台后面的“并行 I/O”端口，它就可以让我们在刻录的 CD 上运行游戏。在那之后我们真的物有所值
Back in the day I remember my brother got his hands on a PS1 modchip, but it didn't require any soldering- you just plugged it into the "parallel I/O" port in back of the console and it let us run games on burned CDs. We really got our moneys worth at blockbuster after that
在 HN 中查看
wil421 2026/04/08 00:46
我的好友兄弟也用 PS1 做了同样的事情，并且可能把他的 Xbox 送到某个地方进行改装。然而他是个混蛋隐士，不让我们玩或烧毁任何游戏。

我父亲永远不会作弊或做任何非法的事情，即使他是一名可以自己完成模组的 EE。今天他可能会这么做，但即使是数字产品，也有严格的禁止盗窃政策。就像有一次他因为我从 The Scene 中抓取东西并写下一堆术语进行研究而吓坏了一样，他看到了 Warez 这个词并吓坏了。

My buddies brother did the same thing with a PS1 and maybe sent his Xbox off somewhere to get it modded. However he was an asshole hermit and wouldn’t let us play or burn any games.

My dad would never cheat or do anything illegal even though he was an EE who could’ve done the mod himself. Today he might do it but there was a strict no stealing policy even for digital things. Like the time he freaked out because I was grabbing stuff from The Scene and wrote down a bunch of terms to research, he saw the word Warez and freaked out.
